name: Physics LLM Presentation Pack
description:
You are an expert academic presentation designer who converts research papers into multiple polished deliverables.

Paper Specification

  • Working Directory: /Users/zhuoran_cisco/Documents/astro-outputs/presentation/physcics-llm-1

  • Source Format: latex

  • Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.13673

  • Code Repository: {{githubUrl}}

  • Custom Requirements: - Use a lot of the figures provided by the paper and you need to first download the LaTeX source from the arxiv website. I think about the logic be very comprehensive and generate about 40 slides including detailed a lot of details in the paper and also be beginner-friendly. Have a background section talking about the setup and the background of transform architecture as well as use the specific about this paper and the context-free grammar which are complicated concepts so give a beginner-friendly but concise introduction with concrete examples and visualizations of examples. For those introductions you need to prepare very good visualizations as well.

Deliverables: slides, blog-post, poster

Configuration

  • Slide Count: detailed

  • Target Audience: researcher

  • Slide Theme: both

  • Blog Style: detailed

  • Poster Orientation: portrait

Skill Reference

This project uses the academic-presentation skill. The skill's SKILL.md contains the complete rendering specification including:

  • 5 HTML slide layout types (title, section-divider, full-bullet, left-right-split, three-zone)

  • Color palette definitions (emph, blue, muted, teal, gold) for light and dark themes

  • Typography rules, box styles, SVG figure guidelines

  • HTMLвҶ’PDF rendering via Playwright

  • Blog Markdown structure and poster CSS grid layout

When source format is PDF or DOCX, the corresponding skill (pdf or docx) is also loaded with format-specific extraction instructions.

The SKILL.md is the single source of truth for all rendering details. Reference it for slide CSS, color hex values, font sizes, and layout dimensions.

Planning Guidelines

Decompose this paper presentation into a DAG of tasks. The output structure depends on which deliverables are selected.

Narrative Arc

All deliverables follow this narrative structure:

  1. Motivation вҖ” Why should the audience care? What gap exists?

  2. Methodology вҖ” What is the proposed approach? Architecture/algorithm.

  3. Results вҖ” Does it work? Quantitative evidence, comparisons.

  4. Discussion вҖ” Implications, limitations, future directions.

Task Structure

Phase 1: Paper Analysis (shared across all deliverables)

  • Obtain the paper source:

    • If a paper link is provided, fetch/download from the link first

    • Read files from the working directory

  • Parse the paper using the appropriate tool for the source format (latex):

    • PDF вҶ’ use the PDF skill for extraction (pypdf, pdfplumber, or the Read tool on .pdf files)

    • DOCX вҶ’ use the DOCX skill for extraction

    • LaTeX вҶ’ read .tex files directly, parse \section, \subsection, equations, figures, and bibliography

    • Markdown вҶ’ read .md files directly

  • Extract structure: sections, equations, figures, bibliography

  • Identify key contributions (3-5 points)

  • If GitHub repo provided, clone and identify key code files

Phase 2: Per-Deliverable Generation (parallel branches)

For slides (when selected):

  • Generate HTML slides using the 5 layout types from the skill

  • Each slide is a self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS

  • Use SVG for architecture diagrams and figure recreations

  • Render to PDF via Playwright headless browser

  • Target slide count: detailed

For blog-post (when selected):

  • Generate Markdown blog post with proper citations

  • Include figure references and equation rendering (LaTeX in Markdown)

  • Style: detailed

For poster (when selected):

  • Generate single-page HTML with CSS grid layout

  • Orientation: portrait (3-col portrait / 4-col landscape)

  • Render to A0 PDF via Playwright

Phase 3: Assembly & Quality Check

  • Verify all outputs render correctly

  • Cross-check content consistency across deliverables

  • Run quality checklist from SKILL.md

Parallelism

  • Paper analysis must complete before any deliverable generation

  • All deliverable branches can run in parallel

  • Assembly depends on all branches completing

Labels

  • l1=Frontend (HTML/CSS rendering, Playwright)

  • l5=Research (paper analysis, content extraction)

Verification

  • "auto" for file creation and rendering tasks

  • "human" for content quality and narrative flow

nodes.title: Narrative Blueprint and 40-Slide Storyboard
nodes.description: Write `outline/narrative-outline.md` with a 500+ word comprehension summary and full 40-slide storyboard following Motivation вҶ’ Methodology вҶ’ Results вҶ’ Discussion. For each slide specify title, layout type (1-5), 3-5 key points, equation plan, figure plan, and transition line. Must include beginner-friendly but concise background section on Transformer setup and CFG concepts with concrete examples/visualization specs.
nodes.content: Write `outline/narrative-outline.md` with a 500+ word comprehension summary and full 40-slide storyboard following Motivation вҶ’ Methodology вҶ’ Results вҶ’ Discussion. For each slide specify title, layout type (1-5), 3-5 key points, equation plan, figure plan, and transition line. Must include beginner-friendly but concise background section on Transformer setup and CFG concepts with concrete examples/visualization specs.

nodes.title: Generate Slides 01-12 (Hook + Background)
nodes.description: Create `output/slides/slide_01.html` through `slide_12.html` using the 5 required layouts and palette/typography rules. Focus on title, motivation, prior gap, and beginner-friendly foundations (Transformer architecture, context-free grammar intuition, concrete parsing examples). Add high-quality custom SVGs for prerequisite concepts and early pipeline visuals.
nodes.content: Create `output/slides/slide_01.html` through `slide_12.html` using the 5 required layouts and palette/typography rules. Focus on title, motivation, prior gap, and beginner-friendly foundations (Transformer architecture, context-free grammar intuition, concrete parsing examples). Add high-quality custom SVGs for prerequisite concepts and early pipeline visuals.

nodes.title: Generate Portrait Poster and A0 PDF
nodes.description: Create `output/poster/poster.html` using portrait A0 3-column CSS grid and render `output/poster/poster.pdf`. Include header, motivation/background, method, key insight, results, discussion, limitations, and QR/citation block. Enforce column balancing (>=95% fill per column) with real content (figures/callouts), not stretched whitespace.
nodes.content: Create `output/poster/poster.html` using portrait A0 3-column CSS grid and render `output/poster/poster.pdf`. Include header, motivation/background, method, key insight, results, discussion, limitations, and QR/citation block. Enforce column balancing (>=95% fill per column) with real content (figures/callouts), not stretched whitespace.

nodes.title: Cross-Deliverable QA and Consistency Gate
nodes.description: Run final quality checks across slides, blog, and poster: narrative consistency, claim-number consistency, equation explanation coverage, figure coverage targets, citation completeness, and rendering sanity checks. Produce `output/qa/report.md` with pass/fail checklist and required fixes if any.
nodes.content: Run final quality checks across slides, blog, and poster: narrative consistency, claim-number consistency, equation explanation coverage, figure coverage targets, citation completeness, and rendering sanity checks. Produce `output/qa/report.md` with pass/fail checklist and required fixes if any.

nodes.title: Bootstrap ArXiv LaTeX Workspace
nodes.description: Initialize the project from arXiv first: download and extract https://arxiv.org/e-print/2305.13673 into `source/arxiv-2305.13673/`, then create working folders `outline/`, `assets/figures/`, `assets/svg/`, `output/slides/`, `output/blog/`, `output/poster/`. Do not start content generation yet. Done means the LaTeX source (including `cfg-ob4.tex` and figure assets) is present and directory scaffolding is ready.
nodes.content: Initialize the project from arXiv first: download and extract https://arxiv.org/e-print/2305.13673 into `source/arxiv-2305.13673/`, then create working folders `outline/`, `assets/figures/`, `assets/svg/`, `output/slides/`, `output/blog/`, `output/poster/`. Do not start content generation yet. Done means the LaTeX source (including `cfg-ob4.tex` and figure assets) is present and directory scaffolding is ready.

nodes.title: Paper Structure and Contribution Extraction
nodes.description: Parse `source/arxiv-2305.13673/cfg-ob4.tex` and extract: section hierarchy, key equations, bibliography signals, and all `\includegraphics` references. Write `outline/paper-structure.md` with 3-5 key contributions and evidence anchors (section/equation/figure refs). If a real GitHub URL is provided (not `{{githubUrl}}`), clone into `external/repo/` and add `outline/repo-notes.md` identifying key code files; otherwise record repo as unavailable in `outline/repo-notes.md`.
nodes.content: Parse `source/arxiv-2305.13673/cfg-ob4.tex` and extract: section hierarchy, key equations, bibliography signals, and all `\includegraphics` references. Write `outline/paper-structure.md` with 3-5 key contributions and evidence anchors (section/equation/figure refs). If a real GitHub URL is provided (not `{{githubUrl}}`), clone into `external/repo/` and add `outline/repo-notes.md` identifying key code files; otherwise record repo as unavailable in `outline/repo-notes.md`.

nodes.title: Figure Inventory and Visual Asset Plan
nodes.description: Create `outline/figure-inventory.md` by mapping paper figures to presentation needs using the rubric (original vs original+annotated vs new SVG). Require a 40-slide-ready plan with at least 22 visuals and >60% content slides visualized. Include per-slide visual tags like `[original: path]`, `[SVG: description]`, or `[none]`. Prioritize original experimental figures and define beginner-focused new SVGs for Transformer basics and CFG parsing examples.
nodes.content: Create `outline/figure-inventory.md` by mapping paper figures to presentation needs using the rubric (original vs original+annotated vs new SVG). Require a 40-slide-ready plan with at least 22 visuals and >60% content slides visualized. Include per-slide visual tags like `[original: path]`, `[SVG: description]`, or `[none]`. Prioritize original experimental figures and define beginner-focused new SVGs for Transformer basics and CFG parsing examples.

nodes.title: Generate Slides 13-28 (Method + Core Evidence)
nodes.description: Create `output/slides/slide_13.html` through `slide_28.html` covering CFG family design, model behavior interpretation, attention pattern findings, and dynamic-programming connection. Reuse many original paper figures (authoritative results/plots) and add SVG simplifications where paper visuals are too dense. Ensure equations are annotated in plain language.
nodes.content: Create `output/slides/slide_13.html` through `slide_28.html` covering CFG family design, model behavior interpretation, attention pattern findings, and dynamic-programming connection. Reuse many original paper figures (authoritative results/plots) and add SVG simplifications where paper visuals are too dense. Ensure equations are annotated in plain language.

nodes.title: Generate Detailed Blog Post
nodes.description: Write `output/blog/post.md` (2000-4000 words) with TL;DR, Motivation, Approach, Key Results, Discussion, References. Include equation blocks with plain-English explanation, quantitative tables, and all figures used in slides (original + new SVGs) with standalone captions. Keep terminology aligned with slide deck.
nodes.content: Write `output/blog/post.md` (2000-4000 words) with TL;DR, Motivation, Approach, Key Results, Discussion, References. Include equation blocks with plain-English explanation, quantitative tables, and all figures used in slides (original + new SVGs) with standalone captions. Keep terminology aligned with slide deck.

nodes.title: Generate Slides 29-40 (Extensions + Discussion)
nodes.description: Create `output/slides/slide_29.html` through `slide_40.html` for robustness, implicit CFGs, PTB/other CFG extensions, uniform-attention findings, limitations, implications, and future directions. Keep researcher-level detail while preserving beginner clarity through recap visuals and concise takeaways.
nodes.content: Create `output/slides/slide_29.html` through `slide_40.html` for robustness, implicit CFGs, PTB/other CFG extensions, uniform-attention findings, limitations, implications, and future directions. Keep researcher-level detail while preserving beginner clarity through recap visuals and concise takeaways.

nodes.title: Assemble and Render Dual-Theme Slide PDFs
nodes.description: Combine all 40 slide HTML files into print-ready combined HTML and render PDFs for both themes: `output/slides/slides-light.pdf` and `output/slides/slides-dark.pdf` (1280x720 pages). Ensure no clipping/overflow and preserve figure legibility. Keep source HTML self-contained and consistent with the skill styling spec.
nodes.content: Combine all 40 slide HTML files into print-ready combined HTML and render PDFs for both themes: `output/slides/slides-light.pdf` and `output/slides/slides-dark.pdf` (1280x720 pages). Ensure no clipping/overflow and preserve figure legibility. Keep source HTML self-contained and consistent with the skill styling spec.

nodes.title: Presentation Pack Complete
nodes.description: Slides (light+dark PDFs), detailed blog post, and portrait poster are complete and quality-reviewed.
nodes.content: Slides (light+dark PDFs), detailed blog post, and portrait poster are complete and quality-reviewed.
